01_initial_request.input_data: FGFR1 rare sequence variants (RSVs) from a cohort of 175 Idiopathic Hypogonadotropic Hypogonadism (IHH) probands
01_initial_request.user_intent: To determine if regional genetic enrichment of missense variants can improve the classification of Variants of Unknown Significance (VUS) under ACMG/AMP guidelines
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Percentage of FGFR1 missense VUS reclassified as pathogenic or likely pathogenic

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The study aims to analyze FGFR1 variants in IHH probands to improve VUS classification. [Gap] Need to identify and process genetic variants from the patient cohort. [Decision] To achieve variant identification, chose to use GATK to perform whole-exome sequencing data processing on IHH proband samples, expecting to obtain jointly called VCF files.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Jointly called VCF files generated from WES data

references.relevance: Describes GATK framework for analyzing next-generation DNA sequencing data

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] VCF files have been generated from WES data. [Gap] Need to analyze and query the variant data. [Decision] To achieve variant analysis, chose to use GEMINI v.0.19.1 to perform integrative database framework analysis on VCF files, expecting to obtain annotated variant database.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Variant database created for downstream analysis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Variant database has been created. [Gap] Need to annotate variants with functional effects. [Decision] To achieve variant annotation, chose to use Ensembl Variant Effect Predictor (VEP) to perform annotation against GRC37/hg19 reference Human Genome, expecting to obtain functionally annotated variants.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Variants annotated with functional effects on FGFR1 protein (822 amino acids)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Variants have been annotated. [Gap] Need to filter for rare sequence variants. [Decision] To achieve RSV identification, chose to use gnomAD browser to perform MAF filtering with threshold <0.1% in all ethnic groups, expecting to obtain rare sequence variants.

parameters.variant_types: non-synonymous variants (stop gain, frameshift, or missense) or canonical splice altering variants ±2 bp from exon–intron boundaries

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 143 FGFR1 RSVs identified in 175 IHH probands (n=95 missense, n=48 protein-truncating variants)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] RSVs have been identified from WES. [Gap] Need to confirm variant calls. [Decision] To achieve variant confirmation, chose to use Sanger sequencing to perform bi-directional confirmation on all RSVs identified by WES, expecting to obtain validated variant calls.

parameters.facility: Center for Computational and Integrative Biology (CCIB) DNA Core Facility at Massachusetts General Hospital

02_agent_trajectory.observation: All FGFR1 RSVs confirmed by Sanger sequencing

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Variants have been confirmed. [Gap] Need initial pathogenicity classification. [Decision] To achieve variant classification, chose to use ACMG guidelines to perform classification as PLP, VUS, or BLB, expecting to obtain initial variant classifications.

parameters.categories_excluded: BP1, PM3, BP3, PM4, PVS1, BP2, BP5, BA1, BS1, BS2, PS4

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 40 classified as PLP, 54 as VUS, 1 as BLB out of 95 missense RSVs

references.relevance: ACMG/AMP standards and guidelines for interpretation of sequence variants

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Initial ACMG classification completed. [Gap] Need computational prediction of variant impact. [Decision] To achieve functional prediction, chose to use PolyPhen2 to perform prediction of RSV impact on protein function, expecting to obtain pathogenicity scores.

parameters.purpose: Prediction of RSV impact on protein function

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Computational predictions generated for missense variants

references.relevance: Predicting functional effect of human missense mutations using PolyPhen-2

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PolyPhen2 predictions completed. [Gap] Need additional computational prediction. [Decision] To achieve functional prediction, chose to use SIFT to perform prediction of RSV impact on protein function, expecting to obtain pathogenicity scores.

parameters.purpose: Prediction of RSV impact on protein function

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Computational predictions generated for missense variants

references.relevance: Predicting amino acid changes that affect protein function

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Initial predictions completed. [Gap] Need composite pathogenicity assessment. [Decision] To achieve variant assessment, chose to use REVEL to perform assessment for differences between variant classes, expecting to obtain composite pathogenicity scores.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: FGFR1 missense VUS reclassified as PLP were more damaging by REVEL than variants remaining as VUS

references.citation: Ioannidis et al. 2016; Tian et al. 2019
references.relevance: REVEL ensemble method for predicting pathogenicity of rare missense variants

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Variant classifications and predictions completed. [Gap] Need to define protein domains for enrichment analysis. [Decision] To achieve domain mapping, chose to use UniProt to perform determination of FGFR1 RSVs domain locations, expecting to obtain domain-specific variant annotations.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: FGFR1 domains identified: D1, D2, D3, TK domains and linker regions (D2–D3, TM–TK)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Domain locations have been determined. [Gap] Need to test for regional genetic enrichment. [Decision] To achieve burden testing, chose to use Fisher's exact test to perform domain-specific burden testing on RSVs in gnomAD and IHH cohort, expecting to obtain enrichment p-values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Significant enrichment found in D1 (p=6.0E-4), D2 (p=3.7E-19), linker D2–D3 (p=1.4E-11), D3 (p=4.1E-16), linker TM–TK (p=1.2E-02), and TK domain (p=2.6E-34)

references.relevance: Burden testing of rare variants identified through exome sequencing via publicly available control data

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Regional enrichment has been defined. [Gap] Need to integrate enrichment data into ACMG classification. [Decision] To achieve VUS reclassification, chose to use regional genetic enrichment as moderate evidence to perform ACMG/AMP classification augmentation, expecting to obtain reclassified variants.

parameters.enriched_regions: D1, D2, D3, TK domains and linker regions (D2–D3, TM–TK)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 37% (20/54) of FGFR1 missense VUS reclassified as pathogenic or likely pathogenic (PLP)

references.relevance: Adaptation and validation of ACMG/AMP variant classification framework by ClinGen's Inherited Cardiomyopathy Expert Panel

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Variants have been reclassified. [Gap] Need to validate reclassification with phenotypic data. [Decision] To achieve phenotypic validation, chose to use Fisher's Exact Test to perform phenotype analysis on non-proband carriers, expecting to obtain correlation between classification and phenotype.

parameters.comparison: non-proband carriers of reclassified PLP vs remaining VUS

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Non-proband carriers of reclassified PLP more likely to express IHH or IHH-associated phenotypes than remaining VUS (76.9% vs 34.7%, p=0.035)

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Phenotypic expressivity analysis in non-proband family members with Fisher's Exact Test

vus_reclassification_rate.interpretation: 20/54 FGFR1 missense VUS reclassified as pathogenic or likely pathogenic using regional genetic enrichment

phenotype_correlation_reclassified_plp.interpretation: Non-proband carriers of reclassified PLP variants expressed IHH or IHH-associated phenotypes

phenotype_correlation_remaining_vus.interpretation: Non-proband carriers of variants remaining as VUS expressed IHH or IHH-associated phenotypes

statistical_significance.interpretation: Significant difference in phenotype expressivity between reclassified PLP and remaining VUS groups

domain_enrichment_d1.interpretation: Significant enrichment of missense RSVs in D1 domain

domain_enrichment_d2.interpretation: Significant enrichment of missense RSVs in D2 domain

domain_enrichment_d3.interpretation: Significant enrichment of missense RSVs in D3 domain

domain_enrichment_tk.interpretation: Significant enrichment of missense RSVs in TK domain

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved: Integration of regional genetic enrichment as moderate evidence into ACMG/AMP guidelines successfully reclassified 37% of FGFR1 missense VUS as pathogenic or likely pathogenic, with reclassified variants showing significantly higher phenotypic expressivity (76.9% vs 34.7%, p=0.035) in non-proband carriers, validating the improved classification accuracy.
